title: astragalus distentus
intro: Astragalus distentus, also known as Locoweed, is a perennial legume plant native to North America. It contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Astragalus distentus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, which are often used to promote immune function, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
